XMUNameFromDriveLetter

The XMUNameFromDriveLetter function retrieves the personalized name associated with the specified mounted memory unit.

DWORD XMUNameFromDriveLetter(
  CHAR chDrive,
  LPWSTR lpName,
  UINT cchName
);

Parameters

chDrive
[in] Drive letter (F through M) of the mounted memory unit to query.
lpName
[in] Pointer to a buffer that receives the personalized name of the memory unit. The buffer must be large enough to contain the returned name, including a terminating null character. A buffer of size MAX_MUNAME characters will be large enough to contain any returned name and terminating null character.
cchName
[in] The size, in characters, of the buffer pointed to by the lpName parameter.

Return Values

If the function succeeds, the return value is ERROR_SUCCESS.

If the function fails, the return value is an error code defined in Winerror.h. The function does not use SetLastError to set the calling thread's last-error code.

Remarks

This function retrieves the personalized name that the user has associated with the specified memory unit (for example, "temp games", "Joes saves", and so on).

The drive letter specified in chDrive must be a drive letter associated with a memory unit previously mounted with XMountMU.

If the buffer specified in lpName is not at least MAX_MUNAME characters in size, and the personalized name of the memory unit is larger than the buffer size, the function will succeed and return only the portion of the MU name that will fit in the buffer.

If the memory unit has not yet been named by the user, the function will succeed and return an empty string.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xbox.h, for include use Xtl.h.
Import Library: Use Xapilib.lib
Unicode: Implemented as Unicode only.

See Also

Memory Unit Functions | XMountMU
